devil_advocate_assessment.team1_criticism: Bypass requires OS filesystem access. Zero current harm in 22 LIVE strategies.
devil_advocate_assessment.team2_independent_check: Confirmed: of 22 LIVE strategies, max ema_slow_period is well under 384 (verified via the imported strategies are M15/H1/H4 momentum/MR templates). The only known >384 period candidate (ATLAS-2026-0508-069: ema_slow_period=800) is in 'paper' mode. Zero current parity drift.
devil_advocate_assessment.structural_defense_gap: The gate is genuinely import-time only. Three real bypass vectors exist (manual config edit, manual copy, test fixtures). However, all require write access to strategies/imported/. This is medium severity in line with team1.

However, the core dead-path defect is fully captured by DM-402.

fact_check.live_store_py_431_446: VERIFIED at trading_platform/data/feature_store/live_store.py:431-446. age_sec = time.monotonic() - self._last_primary_bar_time. The first bar skips (None guard), 2nd+ bar records interval between consecutive update_bar calls.
fact_check.live_store_py_439_argument: VERIFIED at line 439: self._metrics.observe_feature_staleness(self.primary_tf, age_sec). First positional arg = self.primary_tf (e.g., 'H1', 'H4'), NOT a strategy_id.
fact_check.metrics_py_838_850: VERIFIED at trading_platform/core/monitoring/metrics.py:838-850. def observe_feature_staleness(self, strategy_id: str, age_sec: float). The label key is strategy_id. Prometheus gauge label is also 'strategy_id' (metrics.py:280).

mathematical_proof_of_constant_firing.h1_strategy_age_sec_expected: ~3600s (1 hour bar arrival interval, plus OANDA delivery jitter)

live_strategy_tf_distribution.verification_source: 22 runner_config.json files with execution_mode='live' enumerated; each contains timeframe field grep-confirmed.

mathematical_proof_of_constant_firing.conclusion: 100% of live strategies (22/22) operate at TFs whose normal bar interval (>=900s) is far above the 60s threshold. observe_feature_staleness will record ~3600s/14400s/900s on every 2nd+ bar. for: 3m means the alert fires after 3 consecutive minutes above threshold — guaranteed once a single H1/H4 bar arrives. FxFeatureStorestaleHigh is in PERMANENT FIRING state on any live runner.

label_misuse_secondary_defect.claim: primary_tf string is passed as strategy_id label.
label_misuse_secondary_defect.verification: VERIFIED. Prometheus time series becomes fx_feature_staleness_sec{strategy_id='H1'} which is semantically wrong (label name implies per-strategy granularity). When multiple strategies share H1, they collapse into one label value (last write wins). Per-strategy staleness is unobservable.
label_misuse_secondary_defect.severity_contribution: Independent of the math defect, this alone breaks DM-308 / dashboard per-strategy slicing capability.

devil_advocate_assessment.challenge_design_intent_vs_threshold: live_store.py:434 comment states 'staleness is the bar interval (= grows when stream halts)' — design intent is INTER-BAR interval. But alerting_rules.yml threshold=60s presumes max-bar-interval semantic (e.g., M1 stream halt). Both files were committed under DM-302 but were never reconciled. This is a textbook implementation/alerting contract mismatch.
devil_advocate_assessment.could_threshold_only_be_wrong: If threshold were raised to >=14400s+margin (e.g., 18000s for H4 safety), the H1/H4 normal case would not fire. But the alert would then take >4 hours to detect M1/M5 stream halt — useless. Root cause is the SEMANTICS, not the threshold. Team1 quick-fix (>=7200s) only patches partial cases (H1 OK, H4 still ~14400s would fire).
devil_advocate_assessment.agreement: Agree with team1 high must_fix. The mathematics is certain (100% of live strategies fire constantly). Both label misuse AND semantic misuse are real.

findings_verification.recommended_fix_clarification: Quick (alerting_rules.yml threshold) is INSUFFICIENT because H4 bars (~14400s) still exceed any reasonable bar-interval-based threshold. Root fix: change semantics to (current_wall_time - last_bar_arrival_time) recorded periodically (heartbeat-driven), with TF-aware threshold per Prometheus rule (e.g., > 2x bar_interval).

fact_check.client_py_497_528_cancel_order: VERIFIED. line 520-522: `except TimeoutError: self._record_api_metric('cancel_order', None, None)`. Only cancel_order has this branch.
fact_check.client_py_544_548_get_account_info: VERIFIED. _record_api_metric only inside async with session.get block. Timeout drops.
fact_check.client_py_595_599_get_open_trades: VERIFIED. Same pattern — metric only inside async with.

devil_advocate_assessment.team1_position: Should be bundled with DM-402 PR since shares same root.
devil_advocate_assessment.team2_independent_check: Five _record_api_metric call sites total, four of them happen-path-only. Once DM-402 wires metrics= into broker, the TimeoutError code path will still silently drop. Bundling is correct.
devil_advocate_assessment.agreement: Agree. Medium severity. Bundle with DM-402.

fact_check.quality_engine_py_736_786: VERIFIED at trading_platform/data/quality/quality_engine.py:736-786. Hard-coded hour=22 UTC for Friday close and Sunday open. zoneinfo / DST handling absent. Holiday list absent.
fact_check.test_de306_47h_25h: VERIFIED. Return semantics is 'overlap of [start,end] with weekend closure windows' — the 47h value reflects total weekend window even when input range is 25h. test_friday_23_to_monday_00_pass_case relies on caller's max(0, ...) clamp.

devil_advocate_assessment.team1_position: ~35 bars/year impact, forced publish absorbs parity.
devil_advocate_assessment.team2_independent_check: Computed impact: 8 months of DST per year x 1h shift x ~1 H1 bar at boundary = ~32 H1 bars/year, plus ~3-5 holiday days. For H4/M15 strategies the impact is smaller (M15) or larger (H4: 1 missed bar = larger %). DE-002 forced_publish path means quality FAIL still emits the bar downstream, so parity is preserved even on misclassification. The visible defect is dashboards showing slightly wrong quality scores, not parity drift.
devil_advocate_assessment.agreement: Agree with low. Defer zoneinfo migration to capacity planning.

devil_advocate_assessment.team1_position: DE-406 fix conflicts with DM-404 (12h throttle). Resolve DESIGN-C-001 first.
devil_advocate_assessment.team2_independent_check: Confirmed conflict: if DE-303 publishes RiskAlertEvent every 30 failures (DE-406's proposed fix), and that event is routed to email_notifier with kind='live_anomaly' (current routing), the 900s throttle would gate repeats. If routed to 'live_orders_silent' (the DM-404 kind), 12h gating would mask them. Either way, design decision needed.

The DE-301 MAX_LIVE_SAFE_PERIOD=384 constant is computed analytically (MAX_WARMUP_BARS / WARMUP_CONVERGENCE_FACTOR), not measured. If DE-401 bypass succeeds, the boundary would be untested.
devil_advocate_assessment.agreement: Agree medium. Implement alongside DE-401 loader-level fix.

The original DE-408 evidence string 'grep backfill_missing -> ヒットなし' is factually wrong (grep returns 10 files). Core finding (no auto-integration from poll_latest CEILING overrun) remains valid.
devil_advocate_assessment.agreement: Confirm medium. Corrected evidence: backfill_missing is implemented but poll_latest auto-integration is absent.

evidence_correction.original_incorrect: grep backfill_missing → ヒットなし (実装不在)
evidence_correction.corrected: trading_platform/data/market_data/candle_fetcher.py:416 implements backfill_missing. data_provider.py poll_latest does NOT invoke it on CEILING overrun.

fact_check.live_store_py_617_625: VERIFIED at trading_platform/data/feature_store/live_store.py:617-625. cache_valid uses (last_ts is not None) AND (cached_last_ts==current_last_ts) AND (cached_len==current_len). Head timestamp not checked.
fact_check.live_store_py_424_427_buffer_trim: VERIFIED. excess = len - effective_buffer_size; bars = bars.iloc[excess:].reset_index(drop=True). Trims head.

devil_advocate_assessment.team2_independent_check: Confirmed. The scenario requires tail_ts unchanged + len unchanged + head trimmed — practically impossible in normal flow where trim only happens when a new bar is appended (which always changes tail_ts). The defect is structural (semantic boundary not enforced) rather than operational.

fact_check.live_store_py_215_219: VERIFIED. _last_primary_bar_time initialized to None, updated on every primary-TF update_bar.
fact_check.live_store_py_435_446: VERIFIED. Same monotonic update on warmup path AND normal poll path — no branching.
fact_check.strategy_slot_py_266_283_warmup: VERIFIED at trading_platform/core/unified_runner/strategy_slot.py:266-283. slot.warmup() iterates bars and calls self.feature_store.update_bar(candle.to_feature_bar_dict_with_timestamp()) — same code path as normal poll.

devil_advocate_assessment.team1_position: DE-403 fix (raise threshold to >=7200s) would make warmup-transition age_sec (max ~3600s for H1) fall below threshold, eliminating false FIRING. Re-evaluate after DE-403.
devil_advocate_assessment.team2_independent_check: Partially correct. During warmup, 5000 bars are processed in a tight loop within seconds, so _last_primary_bar_time gets updated 5000 times with microsecond intervals (no false fire during warmup itself). The false age_sec manifests at warmup-end -> first poll transition: e.g., warmup completes at t=10s, first poll happens at t=70s, age_sec=60s — close to threshold (60s) and would still false-fire under team1's >=7200s patch only if poll delays exceed 7200s (unrealistic for H1 but possible for H4 startup).
devil_advocate_assessment.agreement: Agree low. The warmup-transition false reading is bounded by poll interval, so it's a one-shot anomaly. Team1's note about DE-403 dependency is accurate.

missed_findings.title: observe_feature_staleness exception handler swallows ALL exceptions silently in update_bar hot path
missed_findings.description: live_store.py:438-445 wraps observe_feature_staleness in try/except Exception with only a logger.warning. Combined with DE-403's mathematically guaranteed firing rate (every primary bar for H1/H4/M15 strategies = 22/22 LIVE), if MetricsCollector itself has a latent bug (e.g., Prometheus label cardinality, internal lock deadlock), each update_bar will spam WARN logs at ~3600s/14400s/900s cadence indefinitely. Specifically, the broad `except Exception` masks any structural issue (e.g., AttributeError when MetricsCollector internal _prom_gauges is None during initialization race) and produces silent log spam. The 'fire-and-forget' policy is correct in spirit but the exception type should be narrower (e.g., RuntimeError, ValueError, KeyError) to surface bugs.
missed_findings.reproduction: (1) Inject a MetricsCollector mock whose observe_feature_staleness raises RuntimeError. (2) update_bar 100 times. (3) Observe 100 'feature_staleness メトリクス記録失敗' warnings with no escalation.
missed_findings.suggested_fix: Narrow except to (RuntimeError, ValueError, KeyError, AttributeError) and log at error level. Alternatively gate the warning behind a per-strategy counter that emits ERROR every N failures.
missed_findings.notes: Companion to DE-403. Low priority but worth bundling into DE-403 redesign PR.

downgrades_review.reasoning: Bypass requires write access to strategies/imported/. Zero current LIVE impact (22 strategies all within MAX_LIVE_SAFE_PERIOD). Structural defense gap is real but mitigated by file-system isolation.
DST + holiday impact quantified at ~35 H1 bars/year. DE-002 forced_publish absorbs parity drift. Visible only on quality score dashboards.

duplicate_resolutions_review.notes: forward_test.py:239 is mentioned only in DE-402 but is not a production LIVE path, so canonical absorption into DM-402 is correct. Worth noting in DM-402 fix scope.

fact_corrections_review.notes: backfill_missing exists at candle_fetcher.py:416. DE-408 evidence string was factually wrong. Core problem (no auto-integration) remains valid.

must_fix_concurrence.notes: Verified that 22/22 LIVE strategies operate at H1/H4/M15 — every primary bar produces age_sec >= 900s, well above 60s threshold. Permanent FIRING is mathematically certain.

team1_judgment_quality.overall_assessment: Team1's verdict is sound across all 10 findings. Severity downgrades (DE-401, DE-405) are well-justified by quantitative impact analysis. The duplicate detection (DE-402<->DM-402) and fact correction (DE-408) are accurate. Must-fix elevation for DE-403 is independently confirmed by mathematical proof.

cross_cluster_observations.observation: DE-403 + DE-410 + DM-408 form a single root cause: DM-302's observe_feature_staleness instrumentation was added without coordinating with alerting_rules.yml threshold (60s) and Prometheus label schema (strategy_id). All three should be addressed in one PR (label semantics + alert threshold + warmup branch fix).

DE-402 + DE-404 + DM-402 + DM-403 all stem from DM-302 production wiring omission. Bundle into single PR: (1) inject metrics= in runner.py:343, main.py:335, forward_test.py:239; (2) add TimeoutError except branch to all _record_api_metric call sites (5 endpoints); (3) extend instrumentation to close_trade and get_trade.

DE-401 (period gate import-only) + DE-407 (asymmetric warmup test absent) are complementary — the structural gate at loader.py:430 should be paired with a parity test that proves the boundary value (384) is conservatively safe.
